commentary_text: The model favors Iran at 49%, while the sportsbook consensus favors Egypt at 41%—a 20-percentage-point gap on the Egypt win probability. The market also prices Iran's win probability substantially lower, at 28%. This divergence could reflect sportsbook awareness of recent lineup changes, injury updates, or current form that the model's last training window did not capture. Alternatively, the gap may signal genuine uncertainty about how these teams will perform in tournament conditions. The dashboard flags the disagreement; no source has a monopoly on accuracy.
